name: Zesty Zucchini Relish
recipeIngredient: 1 large Hot pepper, seeds removed and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Good with hot dogs.
2. Fit food processor with metal blade. Chop zucchini to yield approximately 12 cups and onions to yield about 4 cups. Place the chopped vegetables in a large glass, stainless steel, or enamel bowl. Remove seeds and membranes from sweet green and red peppers. Finely chop the green pepper; cut the red pepper into 1/4 inch cubes; add both to the zucchini.
3. Stir in pickling salt. Let stand overnight.
4. PRESERVING DAY
a. Drain vegetables; rinse with cold running water and drain into cheesecloth-lined colander, twisting cheesecloth to remove excess moisture.
b. Fill boiling water canner with water. Place 6 clean pint mason jars in canner over high heat.
c. Place vegetables in a large stainless steel or enamel saucepan. Stir in remaining ingredients and bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer for 45 minutes until thick.
d. Place snap lids in boiling water; boil for 5 minutes to soften sealing compound.
e. Ladle relish into hot jars, leaving about 1/2 inch of headspace from the jar rim. Remove air bubbles by sliding a rubber spatula between the glass and food; adjust headspace to 1/2 inch. Wipe jar rim removing any stickiness. Center snap lid on jar; apply screw band just until fingertip tight.
f. Place jars in canner. Cover canner; return water to a boil; process for 15 minutes at altitudes up to 1000 feet. Remove jars. Cool for 24 hours. Check jar seals (sealed lids should curve downward). Remove screw bands (if desired); wipe jars, label, and store in a cool, dark place.
Source: Bernardin Guide to Home Preserving
